name: Iridium Communications Inc.
business: Iridium Communications Inc. is a leading provider of global voice, data, and positioning, navigation and timing (PNT) satellite services. It is the only commercial provider of communications services offering true global coverage, connecting people, organizations and assets to and from anywhere, in real time. The company sells its products and services primarily to businesses, U.S. and foreign governments, non-governmental organizations, and consumers via a wholesale distribution network.

exposures.sentence: We provide voice and data communications services to businesses, U.S. and foreign governments, non-governmental organizations, and consumers via our satellite network, which has an architecture of 66 operational satellites with in-orbit spares and related ground infrastructure.

We also utilize our long history operating a commercial LEO satellite system to provide a growing array of engineering and operational services to government customers and government network operators such as the U.S. Space Force.

The U.S. government, directly and indirectly, has been and continues to be our largest single customer, generating $257.0 million in revenue, representing 29% of our total revenue, for the year ended December 31, 2025.
We are a leading provider of global voice, data, and positioning, navigation and timing (PNT) satellite services.

Commercial IoT revenue increased $15.2 million, or 9%, compared to the prior year, driven primarily by a 6% increase in IoT billable subscribers and an increase in a contract with a large customer previously executed in the first quarter of 2024.

In 2024, we acquired Satelles, Inc. (Satelles), a provider of highly secure, satellite-based PNT services that complement and protect GPS and other Global Navigation Satellite System (GNSS) reliant systems.

We are developing a new service called Iridium NTN Direct SM , which we expect to commercially launch from our existing satellite constellation in 2026. Iridium NTN Direct is a 3GPP standards-based service that will provide Narrowband Internet of Things (NB-IoT) and direct-to-device (D2D) connectivity across the globe when fully deployed.
We serve the commercial maritime market with a variety of products, including broadband terminals, embedded devices and handsets.
We are one of the leading providers of mobile satellite communications services to the aviation sector, and we continue to see aviation delivering year-over-year revenue growth.

For example, in September 2025, SpaceX signed an agreement to acquire certain rights and licenses to an aggregate of 50MHz of S-band spectrum, and announced plans to develop a global satellite D2D service using the acquired spectrum.

catalysts.event: Commercial launch of Iridium NTN Direct service
catalysts.sentence: We are developing a new service called Iridium NTN Direct SM , which we expect to commercially launch from our existing satellite constellation in 2026.

catalysts.event: Introduction of Iridium PNT Application Specific Integrated Chip (ASIC)
catalysts.sentence: In 2026, we are introducing an Application Specific Integrated Chip (ASIC) to allow partners to more cost effectively integrate our PNT services into their diverse applications using less power and space.

catalysts.event: EMSS contract expiration with potential renewal or extension
catalysts.sentence: The EMSS contract continues through September 2026 with one six-month extension option exercisable at the election of the U.S. government.

catalysts.event: Expected entry into new EMSS contract with U.S. government
catalysts.sentence: We have begun discussions with the U.S. government on a new EMSS contract, which we expect to enter into later in 2026 or in 2027, prior to expiration.

catalysts.event: SDA contract modification for Ground Management and Integration program
catalysts.sentence: In July 2024, the General Dynamics/Iridium team received a contract modification of $491.6 million for the Ground Management and Integration (GMI) program. Of this amount, Iridium’s share has a value of $240 million over five years.
